summary: Proof prob-01 ('Descriptive Aspects of Structures with Countable Automorphism') is at an early stage, with the verifier assessing only ~10% completeness. The work has a validated 8-vertex path gadget but lacks the substantive argument connecting it to the descriptive set-theoretic claims about countable automorphism groups.
problems: The proof is approximately 90% incomplete per the verifier-agent assessment; the bulk of the argument is missing.
Only the 8-vertex path gadget construction is verified as solid; the remaining reduction and descriptive complexity arguments are unestablished.
No demonstrated link between the gadget construction and the target descriptive-set-theoretic / countable automorphism group properties.
The verifier comment is truncated, so the full set of identified gaps is not yet captured in the report context.
highlights: The 8-vertex path gadget is verified as a solid, critical component with correct construction.
An external verifier-agent has already produced a structured assessment, giving a clear baseline of what is and isn't proven.

suggested_todos.title: Retrieve and document the full verifier-agent comment to enumerate all identified proof gaps
Draft the main reduction argument connecting the 8-vertex path gadget to the countable automorphism group claim
Establish the descriptive complexity (Borel/analytic classification) results that constitute the theorem's core
Define and formalize remaining gadget components needed beyond the path gadget
Schedule a re-verification pass once the main argument draft exceeds 50% completeness
